body_txt: It's no wonder that Playing with Fire is often the first magic power we see in action. It doesn't have Water's or Earth's baggage . It's easier to show than invisible Wind. It's bright, colorful, and clearly powerful beyond regular human ability. Throwing a fireball lets you know that you're in a world of magic and wonder. Typically Fire magic comes first before other magics or a "fireball" spell specifically within the fire class is treated as the basic spell. Bonus points if both are true. There are a few common ways you see this. In a hierarchy of spells, "Fire" or "Fireball" is the basic rank, granted early in the adventure or specifically taught first In-Universe . Foundational before learning advanced fire forms , different Elemental Powers , or wider magic abilities . In many of these depictions, the starter spell itself will be named after some tiny projectile to emphasize its novice level, like fire bolt or flame dart . During early customization , Fire is often touted among the limited list of Starter Mon or Starter Equipment (as long as the list doesn't include a basic form of every ability). Overlapping with Heroism Burns Bright , sometimes the protagonist gets their powers first and they happen to be fire-based. Sometimes Fire isn't the very first spell, but is the first flashy ability. Non-Elemental strikes, shields, physical buffs, or telekinetic powers may come before Fire introduces you to "real" magic. In Real Life , many myths feature The Discovery of Fire as the birth of science and civilization. Making (and snuffing) fire is a versatile survival skill. On the spiritual side, simple fire rituals feature across belief systems. As basic as lighting a candle to strengthen a prayer to burning incense to ward off evil. Scientifically speaking, it's basic physics that just pouring energy into things makes them heat up. All the above could be Mundane Utilities in fantasy, or lead to Stuff Blowing Up via Magic Misfire with novice spell casters. Compare Spell Levels or Elemental Tiers where Fire may be towards the bottom. However just because fire is basic doesn't mean it can't be versatile. Sometimes a character's first and final abilities will both be fire-based even if there are a variety of skills or elements in between.
